cards.back: A programming paradigm that organizes software design around objects and their interactions.

cards.front: What is a class in object-Oriented Programming?
cards.back: A blueprint or template that defines the properties and behaviors of an object.

cards.front: What is an object in object-Oriented Programming?
cards.back: An instance of a class, which possesses its own set of attributes (data) and methods (functions).

cards.front: What is inheritance in object-Oriented Programming?
cards.back: A mechanism by which one class can inherit the properties and behaviors of another class.

cards.front: What is polymorphism in object-Oriented Programming?
cards.back: The ability of an object to take on multiple forms, depending on the context in which it is used.
